title: Metrics Analyst Getting Started Wizard
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want a guided getting-started wizard that walks me through defining a metric, finding related dashboards or reports, and running analysis, so that I can onboard to Metrics Analyst without prior knowledge of the tool.
description:
Implement a step-by-step getting-started wizard for Metrics Analyst to help new users onboard by defining metrics, linking them to Salesforce dashboards/reports, and running their first analysis.

### Wizard Steps

**Step 1 — Define Metric ("Enter Metric")**
Prompt: "What key metric do you track and report in Salesforce?"
- Free-form text input for the metric name (placeholder: e.g., "Revenue")
- Button: "Find Dashboards"

**Step 2 — Find Data Sources ("Select Analytic")**
Pull dashboards and reports from Salesforce, filtered to the top 3 dashboards containing the metric name in the dashboard name or description.
- Show a loading screen with progress messages as data is fetched
- Display a selectable list (single-select only)
- If no dashboards found, show three options:
  1. "None of these relate to my metric" — proceeds to report search
  2. "Locate Dashboards Manually" — loads full dashboard list in data table with search
  3. User can select a matching dashboard

If no dashboards match, show related reports:
- "Relate this Report to my Metric" — creates metric and associates the report (wizard complete)
- "None of these relate to my metric"
- "Locate Dashboards Manually"

**Step 3 — Analyze Metrics ("Explore Metrics")**
When a dashboard is selected, auto-run the Metrics Analyst analysis (Step 2 response).
- Display suggested metrics from the analysis
- Tiles for ignored/suggested categories are removed (too much information on screen)
- Add/merge metric functionality remains as-is from existing Metrics Analyst

**Step 4 — Assign Line of Business**
After analysis, prompt: "Who are the consumers of this metric and analytics?"
- Checkbox list (left-aligned) with existing Lines of Business, suppressing duplicates of: Sales, Marketing, Finance, Executives
- Free-form text input to add new Lines of Business
- If "Executives" is checked, auto-add topic "Executive Reporting"
- Selected LOB applies to all metrics from this analysis

**Completion Flow**
- After wizard completes: "Would you like to add another metric?" → "Yes - Create a New Metric" | "No - View Metrics Glossary"
- If user chooses new metric, restart wizard
- Getting-started screen exits once a dashboard has been related to a metric

### UX Considerations
- Show progressive loading feedback when chaining multiple API requests
- If no dashboards or reports are found, still save the metric to the glossary before restarting
- Metric with no dashboard/report association can be enriched later via a separate wizard
acceptance_criteria: Wizard presents Step 1 with metric name input and Find Dashboards button
Step 2 shows top 3 matching dashboards filtered by metric name
If no dashboards match, system shows matching reports with Relate/None/Locate options
Selecting a dashboard auto-runs Metrics Analyst analysis
Locate Dashboards Manually opens full dashboard list with search
Line of Business step displays existing LOBs with checkboxes and free-form input
Selecting Executives auto-adds Executive Reporting topic
Metrics are saved to glossary even when no dashboard/report match is found
Completion screen offers Create New Metric or View Glossary options
Getting started screen is dismissed after a dashboard is related to a metric
Loading screens display progressive feedback messages during API calls
ux_changes: Multi-step wizard with Define Metric, Find Data Sources, Analyze Metrics, and Assign Line of Business steps. Progressive loading feedback, single-select dashboard/report lists, LOB checkboxes, and completion flow with restart option.
middleware_changes: Chained API requests to fetch and filter dashboards/reports by metric name. Auto-run analysis on dashboard selection. Metric-to-report and metric-to-dashboard association logic. LOB and topic auto-assignment.
data_model_changes: Metric records created in glossary during wizard. Dashboard/report associations stored. LOB and topic relationships created (Executive Reporting auto-added for Executives LOB).
api_changes: Filters dashboard and report queries by metric name. Chains analysis API calls after dashboard selection. Creates metric and association records via existing endpoints.
implementation_notes: Mockup created in Lovable (Vue/inline styles, not Bootstrap). Implementation uses Bootstrap HTML within the existing app. Demo video shared via Loom. Tile display for ignored/suggested categories removed to reduce screen clutter. Merging functionality preserved as-is from existing Metrics Analyst.
comment_summary: Extensive back-and-forth over multiple iterations. Waqar implemented the initial wizard steps and shared a demo video. Ryan provided detailed feedback on labels, loading messages, completion flow, and added a Line of Business assignment step with checkboxes and auto-topic creation. Discussion clarified that metrics should be saved to glossary even with no dashboard/report match, and that the getting-started screen exits only after a dashboard relationship is established.
